results.title: Generation Lab's Terms of Service
results.content:
1.7 "Required Information" is defined in Section 4.3.

1.8 "Health Plan Information" means patient interventions, protocols, and adherence data.

1.9 "Samples" are biological specimens collected via Collection Kits per Generation Lab's instructions.

1.10 "Services" means testing, software, and related products offered by Generation Lab to produce Health Reports.

1.11 "Standard Operating Procedures" or "SOPs" means Generation Lab's instructions for sample handling, patient accounts, and Test performance.

1.12 "Test" means the test described in Exhibit A.

1.13 "Health Report" means the Test results report provided by Generation Lab.

1.14 "Third Party" means any entity other than the Parties or their affiliates. [...] 3.2 Materials. Generation Lab may provide training and promotional materials ("Generation Lab Promotional Materials") for Company's use, subject to approval for modifications and co-branding. Company must comply with branding guidelines and discontinue use upon request.

3.3 Collection Kits. Generation Lab will supply Collection Kits for Test orders, reserving the right to withhold if misused.

## 4. COMPANY RESPONSIBILITIES

4.1 Training. Company will complete Generation Lab's required training programs.

4.2 Marketing and Logistics. Company will educate Providers and Patients, promote Tests per Applicable Laws, and integrate Tests into its systems. Costs are Company's responsibility. Claims about Tests require Generation Lab's consent. [...] 13.8 Support and Resources:

 Educational Resource: Available in the provider portal.
 Support: Please contactsupport@generationlab.com any time - we're pretty good at replying.
 Feedback: All feedback is greatly appreciated! Please help us improve by sending an email to your dedicated point of contact, or book a feedback session and talk to our team ().

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties execute this Agreement by clicking agree on these TERMS OF SERVICE as of the Effective Date.

EXHIBIT A

Test Means: DNA methylation bisulfite sequencing, or other methods used by Generation Lab via proprietary bioinformatics software, producing a Health Report on the Platform or as a PDF.

results.title: InsideTracker Review: Is InsideTracker Legit?
results.content:
## Conclusion

One of the most empowering investments you can make is understanding your health at a deeper level. InsideTracker offers insights from your blood work, but a complete understanding of your cellular and organ-level aging is possible through advanced epigenetic analysis. Don't just track your current biomarkers; instead, get the blueprint to your biological aging trajectory and discover which organ systems need attention before problems become apparent through traditional testing.

© 2025 Generation Lab. All rights reserved. [...] InsideTracker vs. Generation Lab's SystemAge highlights the cutting edge of personalized health testing. While TruDiagnostic offers 11 organ scores, SystemAge analyzes 19 organ systems, offering unprecedented granularity in understanding individual system aging. Based on 20 years of UC Berkeley and Harvard research, SystemAge delivers clinical-grade accuracy in detecting biological age changes, enabling tracking subtle improvements from health interventions. [...] ## What Is InsideTracker and How Does It Work?

InsideTracker is a personalized health and wellness platform that transforms the clinical experience of blood biomarker testing into an accessible, consumer-friendly journey toward optimal health. The service analyzes your blood to measure various biological markers, including familiar ones like cholesterol and glucose to specialized indicators like inflammatory markers and hormone levels, and then translates this data into actionable lifestyle recommendations.

A: Look for key factors: CLIA-certified laboratory, clear collection instructions, proper sample handling, and scientific validation of the testing methodology. Consider whether the test measures actionable biomarkers relevant to your health goals. For standard biomarkers, follow collection protocols precisely. For advanced tests like epigenetic analysis, choose providers with robust scientific credentials and published research backing their methodologies.

By measuring these subtle epigenetic changes, we can transition from reactive healthcare, which responds to problems after they appear, to truly proactive and preventive health optimization. This represents a fundamental shift in our approach to longevity and wellness.

results.title: InsideTracker DNA Test Review: What Does It Tell You?
results.content:
A DNA test gives a static genetic roadmap, while the SystemAge test acts as a dynamic, real-time GPS for your health. This isn't just a clever metaphor; it changes how to approach health optimization and disease prevention.

Generation Lab was co-founded by Dr. Irina Conboy, a UC Berkeley professor called the 'mother of longevity research'. It is backed by 20+ years of research from Harvard and UC Berkeley. SystemAge brings clinical-grade epigenetic diagnostics to your home, making advanced biological age testing accessible.

The SystemAge Advantage: [...] The fundamental question isn't whether genetic testing has value. It does. The question is whether you want to read the manual or drive the car. SystemAge empowers you to move from knowing your risks to improving your biological reality. It transforms aging from a passive process into one you can monitor, optimize, and reverse.

results.title: LetsGetChecked Accuracy: How Reliable is LetsGetChecked?
results.content:
Q: How can I know if my at-home test results are reliable?

A: Look for key factors: CLIA-certified laboratory, clear collection instructions, proper sample handling, and scientific validation of the testing methodology. Consider whether the test measures actionable biomarkers relevant to your health goals. For standard biomarkers, follow collection protocols precisely. For advanced tests like epigenetic analysis, choose providers with robust scientific credentials and published research backing their methodologies.

## Conclusion [...] ### Why Epigenetic Analysis is Important for Longevity

By measuring these subtle epigenetic changes, we can transition from reactive healthcare, which responds to problems after they appear, to truly proactive and preventive health optimization. This represents a fundamental shift in our approach to longevity and wellness.

Key benefits of epigenetic analysis include:

## Generation Lab's SystemAge: LetsGetChecked Alternative

Generation Lab's SystemAge test represents a quantum leap in at-home health testing. It moves beyond standard biomarker analysis into the science of epigenetic aging.

### SystemAge: 20+ Years of UC Berkeley & Harvard Research [...] In contrast, SystemAge by Generation Lab focuses on advanced epigenetics, utilizing over 460 DNA methylation biomarkers with a primary goal of preventive longevity by assessing future risk and biological age. It offers a more detailed look at 19 organ system ages, including cardiac, immune, and neurodegeneration systems. SystemAge answers the question, "How am I aging and what are my future risks?" and provides personalized, organ-specific intervention plans. Its scientific backing stems from over 20 years of UC Berkeley/Harvard Research, notably involving Dr. Irina Conboy, and boasts a clinical-grade accuracy in detecting biological age changes. SystemAge requires a cheek swab sample, has a reporting timeline of 3-4 weeks, and costs $499 for a comprehensive analysis.

### Biological Age Tests: Choosing the Right Kit [...] ## Everylywell vs LetsGetChecked Alternatives

Everlywell and LetsGetChecked serve important roles in reactive health monitoring, but Generation Lab's SystemAge test represents the next frontier in proactive, predictive health testing. SystemAge was co-founded by UC Berkeley's Dr. Irina Conboy, a pioneering longevity researcher, and backed by over 20 years of Harvard and UC Berkeley research. It brings cutting-edge epigenetic science directly to consumers.

What Makes SystemAge Different: [...] Both services provide reliable, professional testing for understanding your health status. They address immediate health concerns and provide insights into specific biomarkers. However, Everlywell and LetsGetChecked are fundamentally reactive tools. They excel at telling you "Do I have a problem now?" or "What are my current levels?" But they cannot answer the more powerful question: "What are my future risks, and how can I prevent problems?"

results.content:
SystemAge offers a high-resolution, 19-system map of your biological age, backed by Ivy League science and decades of peer-reviewed research. It provides actionable, personalized guidance to optimize each system based on your aging patterns. This isn't just another health test; it is your GPS for navigating the aging process.

Stop guessing about your health and hoping generic wellness advice works for your biology. It's time to turn aging into a challenge you can master with precision and confidence. With the right data and targeted interventions, you can control your biological destiny.

### Generation Lab Raises $11M Seed Round as SystemAge™ Becomes World’s Fastest-Growing Longevity Diagnostic [...] Generation Lab is at the forefront of the epigenetic revolution. Co-founded by renowned UC Berkeley professor Dr. Irina Conboy, known as the "mother of longevity," Generation Lab is built on 20+ years of research from UC Berkeley and Harvard. This foundation, combined with cutting-edge laboratory capabilities, led to the SystemAge test, which is the most advanced epigenetic age testing platform available directly to consumers.

## Conclusion

After analyzing GlycanAge test reviews and the science behind biological age tests, the conclusion is clear: GlycanAge is a valid tool for measuring inflammation, but true longevity optimization requires a comprehensive approach. Knowing your inflammatory status is valuable, but it is only one piece of a complex aging puzzle.
